issues: The AI-drafted headline is fabricated and does not match any actual headline text in the OCR. No clear bold headline is visible in the OCR extract.
The long description states Hervey 'fled' Los Angeles — the OCR says he 'hitchhiked north from his broken home in Los Angeles', which is more specific and accurate.
The long description mentions 'plans mentioned involving three teammates' but the OCR cuts off before elaborating (continued on Page 8), so the draft slightly overstates the detail available on p.1.

review.correctedDescriptionShort: Profile of former Richmond Sockeyes defenceman Matt Hervey, now with the Moncton Hawks, who hitchhiked from Los Angeles to British Columbia at age 15 to pursue hockey and escape a troubled home life.
review.correctedDescriptionLong: A front-page feature by Times-Transcript staff reporter Dwayne Tingley profiles Matt Hervey, a third-year defenceman with the Moncton Hawks and former Richmond Sockeyes player, who hitchhiked north from Los Angeles to Langley, British Columbia at age 15 to escape a difficult home situation and pursue hockey. Hervey reflects on how staying in L.A. might have led to a drug habit, and he now works with three teammates to raise awareness about the destructive effects of drugs and alcohol. The article continues on Page 8.
review.notes: No clear headline text is present in the OCR for this article — the AI fabricated one. The byline (Dwayne Tingley) is confirmed accurate. Matt Hervey is a known Sockeyes player (from Whittier, CA), making this archive-relevant. Editor may wish to retrieve Page 8 continuation for a fuller description.
